title: Rogue-Key Attack
description: A rogue-key attack exploits naive public-key aggregation by choosing a related key that cancels another participant's key, making an aggregate signature appear authorized without the intended cooperation. Key aggregation protocols need binding or proof-of-possession defenses.

resources.title: Bitcoin Dev Project: Schnorr rogue-key attack explained
resources.notes: Learner-facing explanation of the key-aggregation attack.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: MuSig
resources.notes: Technical context for key aggregation and rogue-key defenses in multisignature constructions.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Reference background for Schnorr signatures and multisignature coordination.
